analysis_scope: cron-ledger-error.jsonl, glint-monitor-error.log, quant-research-cron-failure.jsonl, gateway.err.log (last 200 lines), workspace/logs/*.log (all ERROR/FAIL/Exception lines)

error_classes.pattern: ProxyError('Unable to connect to proxy', OSError('Tunnel connection failed: 403 Forbidden'))
error_classes.root_cause: HTTP proxy (likely set via environment variables HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY) blocks outbound HTTPS connections to external APIs like glint.trade, kalshi.com, and others. The proxy returns 403 Forbidden for tunneled connections.
error_classes.remediation: Jobs that strip proxy env vars (unset HTTP_PROXY) before running avoid this. Python scripts launched directly inherit proxy settings and fail. Fix: add proxy stripping to all python scripts or remove proxy from agent environment.

error_classes.pattern: payload.model 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' not allowed, falling back to agent defaults
error_classes.root_cause: Deprecated model ID 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' is still configured in 20+ job payloads. The gateway rejects it and falls back to agent defaults, generating a warning on every cron trigger.
error_classes.remediation: Update all job payloads in jobs.json to use an allowed model ID. This generates hundreds of log lines per day and obscures real errors in gateway.err.log.

error_classes.pattern: Research failed (exit 1), Script execution errors, various runtime failures
error_classes.root_cause: Multiple python scripts and shell scripts failing with non-zero exit codes. Common causes include missing dependencies, stale data, and environment issues.
error_classes.remediation: Add structured error logging to all scripts so failures can be categorized. Currently many just log generic 'failed (exit 1)' without details.

error_classes.root_cause: External APIs returning authentication or permission errors. Likely caused by expired API keys, rate limiting, or IP-based blocking.
error_classes.remediation: Audit all API keys for expiration. Implement key rotation and rate limit backoff logic.

error_classes.pattern: Polling stall detected (no getUpdates for Ns); forcing restart
error_classes.root_cause: Long-running agent sessions (some exceeding 15+ minutes) block the Telegram polling event loop. When getUpdates hasn't been called for 300-1000+ seconds, the system forces a restart.
error_classes.remediation: Decouple Telegram polling from agent execution. Run polling in a separate thread/process that is not blocked by agent sessions.

error_classes.pattern: Profile anthropic:default timed out. Trying next account...
error_classes.root_cause: Anthropic API not responding within the timeout window. Often correlated with telegram polling stalls, suggesting the system is overloaded.
error_classes.remediation: Increase timeout windows or reduce concurrent agent sessions. Consider rate-limiting cron job execution to avoid overwhelming the API.

error_classes.pattern: Web fetch failed (403): Cloudflare 'Just a moment...' challenge page
error_classes.root_cause: Target websites (Cloudflare-protected) blocking automated web_fetch requests with 403 and returning challenge pages.
error_classes.remediation: Use headless browser or API endpoints instead of direct web_fetch for Cloudflare-protected sites.

error_classes.root_cause: Agent sessions exceeding maximum lock duration (300-720 seconds). Lock held for 500-1400 seconds in worst cases. Indicates agent tasks taking much longer than expected.
error_classes.remediation: Enforce stricter timeoutSeconds on job payloads. Monitor which jobs are causing long sessions and optimize them.

error_classes.root_cause: Network connectivity issues to Telegram API, possibly caused by DNS resolution failures or temporary network outages.
error_classes.remediation: Already has fallback (autoSelectFamily=false + dnsResultOrder=ipv4first). Transient issue.

error_classes.root_cause: Scheduled tasks reference host filesystem paths that do not exist in the sandbox environment. The .openclaw workspace directory is not mounted in Cowork sessions.
error_classes.remediation: These errors come from legacy sandbox-based execution. Tasks now run directly via Claude Code agent and bypass these scripts.

error_classes.root_cause: Configuration file contains 'mcpServers' key which the config validator does not recognize. Config reload is skipped.
error_classes.remediation: Move mcpServers to the correct configuration location or update validator to accept it.

error_classes.pattern: Could not find the exact text in memory/cron-ledger.jsonl
error_classes.root_cause: Agent attempted to edit a file using stale or incorrect text match. The file content changed between read and edit attempts.
error_classes.remediation: Use append operations instead of edit for log files like cron-ledger.jsonl.

error_classes.root_cause: Agent task exceeded the 10-minute (600000ms) execution limit.
error_classes.remediation: Break long-running tasks into smaller chunks or increase timeout for complex jobs.

critical_findings.top_issue: Proxy blocking accounts for nearly half of all errors. Five components affected. Fix by stripping proxy env vars in all python scripts.
critical_findings.quick_win: Updating model ID from 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' to an allowed model in jobs.json would eliminate 22% of all gateway errors instantly.
critical_findings.systemic_risk: Telegram polling stalls + session lock timeouts + API provider timeouts form a correlated failure chain. When one triggers, the others cascade.
